mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that remains elevated and stable near cycle highs continues to compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 forward returns absent any change in policy stance.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly lower than unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window historically marks an inflection into labor-market deterioration (Sahm-rule-like dynamic), which precedes broad-based earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8 percent near cycle lows leaves limited room before triggering threshold
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the signal's rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and retail sales indicates a synchronized contraction in both durable and non-durable consumer demand, a joint signal stronger than either series alone, which forecasts weaker aggregate corporate revenue and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint-decline episodes produce forward 20d returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction model's out-of-sample rank IC does not exceed the sum of single-series IC contributions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing-sector output and supply-chain throughput, supporting near-term corporate earnings momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Correlation between trailing INDPRO growth and forward 5d returns falls below 0.02 rank IC over a rolling 2-year window, or the sign-hit-rate falls below 50% over 100+ triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated concurrently with a high CPI level, monetary policy is in a restrictive regime that constrains both consumer purchasing power and corporate refinancing, jointly amplifying downside pressure on equities relative to either variable in isolation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33 with CPIAUCSL at 312.23 indicates restrictive stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly more negative than periods with only one condition met (p > 0.05), or interaction-term coefficient is not significant in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the 3-month annualized growth rate of nonfarm payrolls below a critical threshold signals weakening labor demand ahead of visible unemployment increases, leading markets to price in slower consumption and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show forward 20d returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or the signal fails to outperform a naive momentum benchmark on out-of-sample rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts occurring while the federal funds rate is simultaneously elevated reflects credit-driven demand destruction in interest-rate-sensitive sectors rather than a benign supply adjustment, compounding negative wealth effects and forecasting weaker S&P 500 returns over the following quarter specifically under restrictive policy.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1521 thousand units combined with FEDFUNDS at 5.33 percent
failure_condition: HOUST declines under high FEDFUNDS produce forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from HOUST declines under low FEDFUNDS regimes (interaction term p > 0.10), or hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production crossing into negative territory after a period of growth marks an inflection point in the manufacturing cycle that precedes rapid downward earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change crosses from positive to negative
failure_condition: Forward 5d returns following the crossing event are positive in more than 55% of instances, or rank IC of the crossing signal remains below 0.02 across a 3-year rolling walk-forward evaluation.
